element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player repels them with amulet at start; later learns they cannot be defeated by combat alone

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early game with Iolo and Shamino
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Progressive: learn to extract keywords, ask follow-up questions, gather intelligence about Shadowlords through dialogue
Townspeople behavior changes based on which Shadowlord is present; must interview NPCs to learn Shadowlord names
Moongates appear only at night; NPC schedules follow daily routines; affects exploration planning
Player learns to use spyglass/telescope to track Shadowlord movement through planetary orbits
Contains three shards of Gem of Immortality; requires moonstone relocation for optimal moongate access
Flame of Truth (Lycaeum), Flame of Love (Empath Abbey), Flame of Courage (Serpent's Hold); must lure Shadowlords into these to destroy shards
Must be recovered from Underworld and thrown into corresponding Sacred Flames to permanently defeat Shadowlords
Discovered progressively; enable access to new regions
Appear at night; phase-dependent destinations; can be relocated by moving moonstones
Obtained through NPC conversations and side quests; allows scaling of small mountains
Obtained through side quests and multi-NPC information gathering; enables fast terrain traversal and damage avoidance

typical_relief_triggers: Daytime safety (Shadowlords activity affected by time)
Moongate fast travel to known safe zones
typical_tension_triggers: Shadowlord appearance in town (changes NPC behavior: theft, fear, or hostility)
Encounters with monsters in dangerous terrain
Daylight hours when moongates are unavailable
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Investigation-driven with punctuated combat crises. Player alternates between safe NPC interrogation phases and dangerous resource-gathering expeditions. Tension escalates through accumulative environmental hazards (Shadowlord presence affecting each town) building toward climactic Shadowlord confrontations.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Scattered shard locations in Underworld require player agency to discover; multiple paths to Shadowlord confrontation encourage exploration
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Moonstones hidden at moongate locations can be relocated; multi-NPC dialogue reveals hidden item locations (grapple, magic carpet); planetary telescope access requires specific location knowledge

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate: Player repels Shadowlords at start but immediately learns combat alone cannot defeat them; introduces NPC conversation dependency
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating: Shadowlord presence mechanics create dynamic threat in towns; requires synthesis of NPC intel, shard location discovery, and dungeon navigation
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex synthesis: Player must coordinate three-pronged Shadowlord confrontation strategy (learn name via NPC + recover shard from Underworld + reach Sacred Flame + perform ritual)
notable_spikes: Initial Shadowlord encounter establishes that normal combat fails
Each Shadowlord discovery and tracking phase
Underworld exploration with multiple shard locations to navigate
Final three Shadowlord confrontations requiring flame-based ritual combat

notable_level_patterns.description: Safe hub areas where player interrogates NPCs for Shadowlord information. Shadowlord presence in town changes NPC behavior (theft, fear, hostility), creating dynamic threat without direct combat. Player learns names and characteristics through conversation keywords.

Expanded dungeon system requiring navigation to three shard locations. Player must manage resources, avoid or overcome monsters, and locate specific artifact pieces. Moongates can be relocated to optimize access patterns.

End-state ritual locations (Lycaeum, Empath Abbey, Serpent's Hold) where player summons specific Shadowlord by name, lures it into flame, and destroys corresponding shard. Combines combat pressure with environmental puzzle elements.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Flame of Truth, Flame of Love, Flame of Courage

notable_level_patterns.description: New movement tools (horses, ships, grapple, magic carpet) acquired through conversation chains and side quests unlock access to previously impassable terrain or enable faster traversal of established routes.
